title: Time-to-First-Ride by Signup Cohort

landmines.name: no-completed-filter
landmines.kills: Using MIN(request_ts) over all statuses treats a rider's earliest cancelled/no_driver request as their 'first ride', pulling averages down and counting riders who never actually completed a ride.

landmines.name: first-by-trip-id-not-time
landmines.kills: Picking the first completed trip by MIN(trip_id) / ORDER BY trip_id is wrong because trip_id is not monotonic with request_ts; the earliest-id completed trip is often not the earliest-time one, so the days gap is taken from the wrong row (20 riders affected on the visible sample).

landmines.name: request-ts-ties
landmines.kills: At purple+ scale many rows share an exact request_ts second for a rider; ORDER BY request_ts alone leaves 'first' non-deterministic, so the tiebreak on trip_id is required.

notes: Purple: first-per-group via ROW_NUMBER + a cohort average with a date difference. Portable pieces: SUBSTR month key, ROW_NUMBER window. The whole-day gap is the one dialect-specific piece: reference.sql is SQLite (julianday); reference.postgres.sql (date subtraction), reference.mysql.sql (DATEDIFF), reference.duckdb.sql (DATE_DIFF) are provided. mssql: month via FORMAT(signup_date,'yyyy-MM'), gap via DATEDIFF(day, signup_date, request_ts). Only the SQLite reference is self-verified in-sandbox; the day-count is identical (integer days) across engines.
